name: Himinbjorg
description: Himinbjorg, meaning "heavenly fortress" or "sky cliffs," is the home of the god Heimdallr in Norse mythology. Located at the edge of Asgard, where the burning Bifrost bridge, the rainbow bridge connecting Midgard (Earth) and Asgard, meets the sky, Himinbjorg serves as Heimdallr's watchtower. From this high vantage point, Heimdallr can see and hear everything that happens in all nine realms, allowing him to guard Asgard from giants and other threats. He is ever vigilant, requiring little sleep and possessing exceptional senses. Himinbjorg is described as a magnificent and well-fortified structure, befitting its crucial role in the defense of the gods. The constant presence of Heimdallr at Himinbjorg highlights the importance of vigilance and protection in Norse cosmology. The sounds of Heimdallr's horn, Gjallarhorn, echoing from Himinbjorg, signal the approach of Ragnarok, the end of the world, alerting the gods to prepare for the final battle. Its location at the Bifrost underscores the interconnectedness of the realms and the constant threat from the outside.
subtitle: Heimdallr's Heavenly Fortress on Bifrost

keyMyths.title: Heimdallr's Watch
keyMyths.description: Heimdallr resides in Himinbjorg, constantly watching for threats to Asgard. His keen senses allow him to see and hear everything across the nine realms.

keyMyths.title: Gjallarhorn's Call
keyMyths.description: From Himinbjorg, Heimdallr will blow the Gjallarhorn to warn the gods of Ragnarok. This signal will gather the Aesir for the final battle against the forces of chaos.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, detailing the structure of the cosmos and the roles of the gods.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, detailing the structure of the cosmos and the roles of the gods.
